---
title: "3. Balance Tests"
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")
import utils

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
from scipy import stats
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf

utils.set_theme()

dfs = utils.load_all()
baseline = dfs["baseline"]
schools  = dfs["schools"]

# Merge treatment arm into baseline
treatment_lookup = schools[["school_id", "treatment", "treatment_label"]].copy()
baseline = baseline.merge(treatment_lookup, left_on="school_code", right_on="school_id", how="left")

# Flat list of (col, label) for balance vars
balance_cols = {col: label for section in utils.BALANCE_VARS.values() for col, label in section.items()}
```

## 3.1 Covariate Balance Table

Mean (SD) by arm. P-values are from pairwise two-sample t-tests. Standardized mean differences (SMD) are Cohen's d relative to Control.

```{python}
def cohens_d(group_a: pd.Series, group_b: pd.Series) -> float:
    """Pooled-SD standardized mean difference."""
    na, nb = len(group_a.dropna()), len(group_b.dropna())
    if na < 2 or nb < 2:
        return np.nan
    pooled_std = np.sqrt(
        ((na - 1) * group_a.dropna().var() + (nb - 1) * group_b.dropna().var()) / (na + nb - 2)
    )
    if pooled_std == 0:
        return np.nan
    return (group_a.dropna().mean() - group_b.dropna().mean()) / pooled_std


arms = list(utils.TREATMENT_MAP.values())  # Control, T1, T2
groups = {arm: baseline[baseline["treatment_label"] == arm] for arm in arms}
control = groups[arms[0]]

rows = []
for col, label in balance_cols.items():
    if col not in baseline.columns:
        continue
    row = {"Variable": label}

    for arm in arms:
        data = groups[arm][col].dropna()
        if set(data.unique()) <= {0, 1, 0.0, 1.0}:
            row[arm] = f"{data.mean()*100:.1f}%"
        else:
            row[arm] = f"{data.mean():.2f} ({data.std():.2f})"

    # Pairwise t-test p-values vs Control
    for arm in arms[1:]:
        a = control[col].dropna()
        b = groups[arm][col].dropna()
        if len(a) >= 2 and len(b) >= 2:
            _, pval = stats.ttest_ind(a, b, equal_var=False)
            row[f"p (Control vs {arm})"] = f"{pval:.3f}"
            row[f"SMD (Control vs {arm})"] = f"{cohens_d(b, a):.3f}"
        else:
            row[f"p (Control vs {arm})"] = "—"
            row[f"SMD (Control vs {arm})"] = "—"

    rows.append(row)

balance_table = pd.DataFrame(rows)
print(balance_table.to_string(index=False))
balance_table.to_csv("../outputs/tables/03_balance_table.csv", index=False)
```

## 3.2 Joint F-test (Omnibus Balance)

Regress each treatment indicator on all available baseline covariates. A significant F-statistic suggests baseline covariates jointly predict treatment assignment, which would indicate a balance concern.

```{python}
available_cols = [col for col in balance_cols if col in baseline.columns]
formula_vars = " + ".join(available_cols)

for arm_label, arm_code in [("T1", 1), ("T2", 2)]:
    df_sub = baseline[baseline["treatment"].isin([0, arm_code])].copy()
    df_sub["is_treated"] = (df_sub["treatment"] == arm_code).astype(int)

    # Drop rows with any missing value in the covariates
    df_clean = df_sub[["is_treated"] + available_cols].dropna()
    formula = f"is_treated ~ {formula_vars}"

    try:
        model = smf.ols(formula, data=df_clean).fit()
        fstat = model.fvalue
        fpval = model.f_pvalue
        n     = int(model.nobs)
        print(f"\nControl vs {arm_label}  (n={n})")
        print(f"  F-statistic: {fstat:.3f}   p-value: {fpval:.4f}")
        if fpval < 0.05:
            print("  ⚠  Significant — baseline covariates jointly predict treatment.")
        else:
            print("  ✓  Not significant — randomization appears balanced.")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  Could not fit model for {arm_label}: {e}")
```

## 3.3 Love Plot (Standardized Mean Differences)

Visualizes the magnitude of imbalance for each covariate. Values inside the dashed lines (|SMD| < 0.1) indicate good balance.

```{python}
smd_rows = []
for col, label in balance_cols.items():
    if col not in baseline.columns:
        continue
    for arm in arms[1:]:
        a = control[col].dropna()
        b = groups[arm][col].dropna()
        d = cohens_d(b, a)
        smd_rows.append({"Variable": label, "Arm": arm, "SMD": d})

smd_df = pd.DataFrame(smd_rows).dropna(subset=["SMD"])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, max(5, len(balance_cols) * 0.45)))

colors = {"T1 — Standard AI": "#DD8452", "T2 — Modified AI": "#55A868"}
offset = {"T1 — Standard AI": -0.15, "T2 — Modified AI": 0.15}

variables = smd_df["Variable"].unique().tolist()
y_pos = {v: i for i, v in enumerate(variables)}

for arm, color in colors.items():
    sub = smd_df[smd_df["Arm"] == arm]
    ys = [y_pos[v] + offset[arm] for v in sub["Variable"]]
    ax.scatter(sub["SMD"], ys, color=color, label=arm, s=60, zorder=3)

ax.axvline(0,    color="black", linewidth=0.8)
ax.axvline( 0.1, color="gray",  linewidth=0.8, linestyle="--")
ax.axvline(-0.1, color="gray",  linewidth=0.8, linestyle="--")
ax.set_yticks(list(y_pos.values()))
ax.set_yticklabels(list(y_pos.keys()), fontsize=9)
ax.set_xlabel("Standardized Mean Difference (Cohen's d)\n(Treatment − Control)")
ax.set_title("Love Plot — Covariate Balance by Treatment Arm")
ax.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/03_love_plot.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

## 3.4 Summary

```{python}
# Count variables with |SMD| > 0.1 per arm
for arm in arms[1:]:
    sub = smd_df[smd_df["Arm"] == arm]
    n_imbalanced = (sub["SMD"].abs() > 0.1).sum()
    n_total = len(sub)
    print(f"{arm}: {n_imbalanced}/{n_total} variables with |SMD| > 0.1")
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Interpretation guidance:** An SMD > 0.1 or < −0.1 is typically considered a meaningful imbalance in RCT evaluations. If several variables are imbalanced, downstream impact analyses should include those variables as controls. A significant joint F-test (p < 0.05) warrants the same precaution even if individual SMDs look small.
:::
